title: Velvety Sweet Algerian Pumpkin Tagine
description: A silky, mildly sweet pumpkin tagine enriched with rosemary and tart pickles, ideal for a post-workout snack thats low on sugar but high on flavor.

ingredients.name: pumpkin (peeled and cubed)
ingredients.notes: use a sweet variety like butternut or acorn

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium‑sized tagine or heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, cinnamon, salt, and pepper, and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the pumpkin cubes, paprika, and rosemary sprigs, then pour in the almond milk. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes or until the pumpkin is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, toast the pickle slices lightly in a dry skillet for 1–2 minutes to mellow their tartness.
Optional: blend a small portion of the cooked pumpkin mixture with the almond milk and strain to achieve a smooth, velvety consistency before adding back to the pot.
Gently fold the pickles into the tagine, then simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove the rosemary sprigs, taste, and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve warm, garnished with a drizzle of olive oil if desired.
Enjoy immediately as a sweet, protein‑rich post‑workout snack.
